evidence.observed: practice run showed upstream_unavailable in check_stock/get_discount/calc_shipping and diacritic destinations failing

findings.root_cause: tool_error_rate was high and normalize_unicode was disabled, so valid products or destinations could be treated as unavailable.
findings.suggested_fix: Enable retry, set tool_error_rate to 0 for the submitted config, normalize unicode, and retry once in wrapper when a tool returns upstream_unavailable.

evidence.observed: check_stock was called five times for the same item in one request

findings.root_cause: The default prompt and disabled loop guard encouraged repeated tool calls instead of using the first verified observation.
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite the prompt to allow each tool at most once, enable loop_guard, cap tool_budget, and have the wrapper compute from the first verified trace.

evidence.observed: answers repeated customer email and phone numbers

findings.root_cause: The prompt did not forbid echoing customer contact data and redaction was disabled.
findings.suggested_fix: Enable redact_pii, strip PII before calling the agent, and redact the final answer in wrapper.

evidence.observed: some answers said a verified in-stock product was not found after a later failed repeated tool call

findings.root_cause: High temperature and weak prompt let the model ignore earlier verified tool observations and overreact to later failures.
findings.suggested_fix: Lower temperature, verify arithmetic, and recompute subtotal, discount, shipping, and total from the tool trace in wrapper.

evidence.observed: README/RULES state private orders include fake system prices or instructions in notes

findings.root_cause: The original prompt did not treat order notes as untrusted data.
findings.suggested_fix: System prompt explicitly ignores note instructions and wrapper removes note-like injected suffixes before routing to the agent.
